entry.title: Re: Onboarding Dashboard (New) in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding: H2 2020
entry.content:
Hello,

If I cancel a candidate from onboarding in the ONB dashboard, where can I find the candidate details? I did a search for them in EC as an inactive user and I do not see them listed there. Can someone let me know where to find the candidate?

Thank you

entry.title: TTA for SAP integrated with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Here are some questions about the usage of the Test automation Tool from Tricentis:

- I would like to know if there are already predefined (created) test scripts available in TTA, if well where can we find it? I already looked in TTA itself and I didn't find it...

- Can we use the Best practices from SAP and import it in TTA in order to automatically create the Test scripts from scratch ?

- If none of above do we always need to create a test script from scratch in TTA?

- Right that there are 5 Tricentis license in CALM, what are limitation on it?

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Search Help Exit with own Selection and Dialog to choose a Value returning blank values
entry.content: Don't use a search help, don't use a search help exit, don't use ABAP code, just enter the domain values in the domain.

entry.title: Re: Calling a BAPI inside a BADI
entry.content: that question is pretty much nonsense, if I'm not wrong.

entry.title: How to set Flat Hierarchy with a switch when 'Show only leaves' is activated
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm working in SAP Analytics cloud and I'm trying to create a switch that changes all the row dimensions' hierarchies to FlatHierarchy. The issue I'm facing is that if one (or more) of these dimensions has already the 'Show only leaves' option activated, my script does not work.

This is my script:

if (Switch_1.isOn()){
Table_1.getDataSource().setHierarchy("Dimension1",Alias.FlatHierarchy);
Table_1.getDataSource().setHierarchy("Dimension2",Alias.FlatHierarchy);
Table_1.getDataSource().setHierarchy("Dimension3",Alias.FlatHierarchy);
Table_1.getDataSource().setHierarchy("Dimension4",Alias.FlatHierarchy);
}

If all dimensions are in the 'All' hierarchy, when I click on the switch it works, but if I have already selected 'Show only leaves' in any of the dimensions it does not work and throws this message "hierarchyId is undefined when process member navigation filter:(...)" ¿Is there any way of doing this?

entry.title: Re: Change the tax code validity period in Define Tax Cod...
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm not sure what you mean with data migration. From my understanding, you must activate Time-Dependent Taxes (TDT) for a target country/region in your system first. Once TDT is active, you can maintain tax codes and tax rates using the Define Tax Codes for Sales and Purchases configuration activity. 

Best regards,

Anja Danneberg, SAP

entry.title: Work Center Scheduling
entry.content:
Hi,

I manage a workcenter that operates with a variety of configurations and arrangements. My goal is to streamline the scheduling process by designating Thursdays for processing jobs that include "head" in the material description. To achieve this, I need SAP to allocate time at that specific workcenter for these particular jobs exclusively on Thursdays. What steps should I take to implement this scheduling adjustment within SAP??

entry.title: I am getting the error : 'Artifact upload failed due to an unexpected error.'
entry.content:
I am getting the error : 'Artifact upload failed due to an unexpected error.' when I am trying to upload any zip file of iflow. I have downloaded one iflow in a zip format and from one tenant and trying to upload that same zip file to another tenant. Then I am getting this error. 

Can you please help me on that?

entry.title: Register Now – Q3 Recruiting User Group Meeting - Sept 26th
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the option "Yes" to "Will you be attending?" will NOT register you for the event. To register for the event, please Register Here to receive the event invite and login details.

Q3 Recruiting User Group Meeting

Thursday, September 26th - 11:00 am - 12:30 pm Eastern

REGISTER NOW!

Our Recruiting User Group is customer led by Purdue and Nestlé. This meeting will provide you with an opportunity to connect with peer Recruiting customers, submit questions to chat with others about, hear from Recruiting experts and listen to a Customer Spotlight.

If you haven’t already, please subscribe to the Customer Empowerment Events Newsletter HERE!  It’s a great resource to stay up to date on all User Group sessions globally.  Also, please check out the User Group Calendar HERE!

Any questions about this meeting, please reach out to Lindsay Westra, Director, User Engagement, SAP SuccessFactors at lindsay.westra@sap.com.  Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Intent based navigation - open target app in p...
entry.content: i don't think you need intent based navigation

entry.title: Re: Currency Setting in S/4HANA 2021.
entry.content:
Hi Benard,

Exchange rate is picked based on translation date (WWERT). Without any enhancement, Translation date is equal to Posting date (BUDAT). But if you want different date as translation date for example document date (BLDAT) as translation date then please use BADI =FI_TRANS_DRIVE_WWERT with FM = FI_DERIVE_WWERT. Hope this helps.

BR,

Harihar Prasad Kewat

entry.title: add/ read attachments of freight order
entry.content:
Hey there!

How do I add attachments to a freight order and how do I read them?

in R/3 ECC attachments have been added via Generic Object Services (GOS).

After I added an attachment to a freight order, there is no data stored in table SRGBTBREL. Therefore I think that a different technique is used here.

Thanks for any hint or advice!

Cheers
~Enno

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hi All

 

My Name is Blessing.

Single mom, mid career transition. Ethnic minority in remote area of the UK.

I am looking to transition to become an SAP Consultant and this is a great opportunity for me to upskill and grow.

entry.title: Re: SFTP Sender Adapter - Multiple Java Nodes
entry.content: Did you get an answer to this? We are also facing the same issue intermittently.

entry.title: Buchungskreisübergreifende Erlösführende Projekte
entry.content:
Hello,

 

we´re creating Revenue Relevant projects in Public cloud. It seems it is not possible to assign different Company Codes (respectively different cost center from different company codes). All are in one Controlling area. Is that not possible in Public cloud? I know that setup from on-Prem Systems.

Does someone have any experience with that?

Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Cross-company code revenue-generating projects
entry.content:
Hello,

 

we´re creating Revenue Relevant projects in Public cloud. It seems it is not possible to assign different Company Codes (respectively different cost center from different company codes). All are in one Controlling area. Is that not possible in Public cloud? I know that setup from on-Prem Systems.

Does someone have any experience with that?

Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Re: add/ read attachments of freight order
entry.content: Generally speaking, S/4HANA continues working with GOS a lot...

entry.title: Functional Area Usage
entry.content: I am working on restructuring my organizations functional areas. Currently, they are used to identify a detailed program, which constitutes a specific phase or segment of operations. They are currently two-digit alpha-numeric codes. I am trying to restructure the FA to identify organizational missions and associated sub-goals. Is there a preferred way to do this? Currently, I am thinking on identifying a new element to house the current functional areas which offer the same functionality of as functional area. I have done some initial research on Financial Management Area as a potential solution since it is also in the FM module. Would this approach work? Are there any other suggestions on identifying mission and goals in the functional area vs the financial management area or vice versa?

entry.title: Re: Check change history for the customer master
entry.content:
Hi Johan,

there is an app Display Changes to Customers.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3074615/E

Regards,

Doreen

entry.title: Re: Get Delivery Header Text in S4HANA Cloud custom CDS view

@shashankagg ,

Not sure of the which field you wanted in the CDS view. Did you get a chance to look at the released CDS view in the App : View Browser for example : I_DeliveryDocument Example as below. Hope this helps.

Best Regards,

Sri

entry.title: Re: MDG DQM:Employing rules in central governance & mass processing in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi All,

"additional section" in the rule definition, for what is it used?

Something with central governance but I 'm not able to find a document explaining the use of it.

Could you help me?

 

Thank you

 

Regards,

Matteo

entry.title: Withdrawing stock from material with material movement 221 shows zero
entry.content: While withdrawing stock for material  through movement type 221 the amount is shown as zero. Reservation  has WBS.. Expected is that amount should be shown when withdraw is done.. Could you please clarify if the amount is impacted based by the WBS element

entry.title: Re: New to the community
entry.content: Hi JP! Glad to have you on SAP Community and I look forward to learning more about your experiences on SAP Business Network. 🙂

entry.title: Re: Print format in Cloud Public
entry.content:
Hello @Pepe-Rivero 

 

The Print Formats supported in S/4HANA Cloud is documentation at Creating Print Queues

 

Format: The format is the printer language type of the queue. The following language types are available: PDF, CAB 203 dpi, CAB 300 dpi, Datamax 203dpi, Datamax 300 dpi, Datamax 406 dpi, Datamax 600 dpi, HP Laserjet 4350 PCL 5e, HP LaserJet 4350 PS, PCL 5c (Color), PCL 5e (Monochrome), Intermec 203 dpi, Intermec 300 dpi, Intermec 400 dpi, Lexmark T644 PCL 5e, Lexmark T644 PS, PostScript 2, Postscript 3, Toshiba 203 dpi, Toshiba 305 dpi, XMLDATA, Zebra 203 dpi, Zebra 300 dpi, Zebra 600 dpi.

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: OOABAP
entry.content:
Hello,

The right sequence is:

1.) Static Constructor of super1.

2.) Static Constructor of sub1.

3.) Instance Constructor of sub1

4.) Instance Constructor of super1 finally!

Refer the below SAP link for better understanding,

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/acquire-core-abap-skills/implementing-inheritance_bfdb59f7-0f99-48b9-b019-a7b766830ecc

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Re: Get Delivery Header Text in S4HANA Cloud custom CDS view
entry.content: I wanted custom CDS view where all long texts are stored for delivery/sales order header and line items..

entry.title: Start from Basics, Zero to Hero with SAP BTP CAP Node JS Part 1
entry.content:
Welcome to the First Blog: SAP CAPM Full Stack UI5 Application with CRUD Operations. Till now most of us would already have the theoretical knowledge of the CAPM or Cloud Application Programming Model. As there are a lot of blog post already available on CAP Model in the Community. So, we are cutting it short and skipping to the actual stuff.

Click Here 

  • You already have an SAP Account created with your Email ID.
  • You already have an SAP BTP Trial Account.

Step 1: Log in to your Trial Account via the below link:

Hana OnDemand Trail Account link 

You must see the below screen after logging in using your credentials.

 

Step 2: Open SAP BAS

Click on SAP Bussiness Application Studio Links under Quick Tool Access. You will see the below screen with Privacy Statement regarding the usage rules & regulations of using BAS Trial Version.

You can skip & click OK to continue, which will land you on the below page.

Step 3: Create your Dev Space

Click on Create Dev Space

 

You will see the below screen with a lot of Dev Space Options.

Choose Full Stack Cloud Application Dev Space & give it a name. We are naming it DEV_BTP. Now click on Create Dev Space button to create your Space.

You will see the below screen, your space will be in Starting Status.

Don't worry it may take some time to start depending on the load on the trial server. Finally, you will get something like below, your Dev Space will be in Running Status & your setup is complete.

Feel free to drop your comments in the comment section.

Further reading - https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/

Next Blog

entry.title: Start from Basics, Zero to Hero with SAP BTP CAP Node JS blog Part 2
entry.content:
Welcome to the Second Episode of the Series: SAP CAPM Full Stack UI5 Application with CRUD Operations. Till now we have created Development Space in BAS and completed the required setup. In this episode, we will create New Project in our Space that will be used for further development.

Assumption(OR Must Have to Start):

  • You have followed our previous episode  to set up the Development Space or already have the required Space ready with you.

Step 1: Log in to your Trial Account & open BAS


You must see the below screen after logging in using your credentials.

The Dev Space is always in stopped space, you can think of it as a Virtual Machine. When it's not in use it automatically stops & you will have to start the same to get going. Click on the play to start your Development Space.

Wait until the running phase completed.

Don't worry too much, the start time is not dependent on your internet but BAS server load.

Finally, it started & we are good to go now.


Step 2: Open your Dev Space navigate to Project Work Space


You can bookmark this page to easily access all your Dev Spaces.

Click on the DEV Space name to open the Dev Space.

It may say One Moment, but you may go & get some coffee while it loads.

OHK, Finally it opened, You can bookmark this as well to directly access your Specific Development Space.

Now let's navigate to Project WorkSpace. Click on file & choose Open Workspace from the drop-down list.

You will get the below screen. Choose projects & click open.

Now we are in the project workspace. Let's open a terminal and proceed further. Click on Terminal & choose  New Terminal.

You will get the below terminal screen. Now copy the below command & run it in your terminal. This will create a Project with the name: cap_tutorial.

mkdir cap_tutorial

You will see that a new project folder is created with the name cap_tutorial. You the below command to navigate to this new folder in the terminal.

cd cap_tutorial

 

You will be switched to the created project folder cap_tutorial. Copy the below command to create the CAPM project.

cds init

 

The creation will start in our project folder & let's wait for the build.

Cool, now you have your project ready. The structure should be like the below screen.

Feel free to drop your comments in the comment section.

In this blog post, we have learnt how to set up Project in dev-space in Business Application Studio(BAS).

In the next blog post, we will see how to create Database Entities on BAS.

Further reading – https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/

entry.title: When to use experts - and when NOT to
entry.content:
I always say that one of the best things about being an EA is that we don’t have to be an expert in everything. We just have to be familiar enough with a broad set of topics and then bring everything together in a strategic way.

With that in mind, check out this TED Talk about When To Use Experts (and when NOT to). The video is somewhat dated (I’m guessing 2008-2011 timeframe based on the references used) but helped me think about the role and importance of proper domain expertise in EA conversations.

Whether you see yourself as an expert or must respond to your (internal/external) client’s request to bring expertise to a conversation, this talk explains an interesting perspective that might change how you see your role in those conversations.

My top takeaways:

  • When listening to advice from an expert, our brain’s decision-making areas naturally “shut off”.
  • Be ready to take experts on – prepare yourself to get the most out of their expertise (e.g. speak the language, get a foundational understanding before the meeting, etc.)
  • Be a rebel about the expert’s assumptions / methodologies. Exercise “managed dissent”.

entry.title: Why Business Need To Adapt a Clean Core Design?
entry.content:
The SAP Clean Core principle focuses on maintaining a pristine and unaltered SAP ERP core system by leveraging standard functionalities and minimising custom code within the core environment. Here’s an illustrative model of the dimension of SAP Clean Core design and an in-depth look at the key aspects and practices involved in maintaining a clean core.

 

1. Minimising Custom Modifications:

  • Standard Functionality Adoption: Prioritise the use of SAP’s standard features and best practices instead of custom developments. SAP’s standard functionalities are designed to be robust, scalable, and secure, covering a broad range of business processes.
  • Configuration Over Customisation: When specific business requirements need to be addressed, configure the existing SAP functionalities rather than customising the core code. Configuration is easier to manage and maintain compared to custom code.

 

2. Utilising Extensible Frameworks:

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Leverage BTP to build extensions and custom applications side-by-side rather than embedding them directly into the core. BTP provides tools and services to develop, integrate, and manage these extensions.
  • In-App Extensibility: Utilise in-app extensibility options like SAP Fiori apps and custom fields within the SAP S/4HANA system to achieve personalisation and minor customisations without altering the core system.

 

3. Side-by-Side Extensions:

  • Micro-services and APIs: Develop extensions as micro-services and use APIs to connect these services to the SAP core. This approach ensures that custom functionalities are isolated and do not interfere with core operations.
  • Cloud-Native Developments: Implement cloud-native solutions on BTP that can interact with the SAP core via standard integration points, ensuring scalability and maintainability.

 

4. Adopting a Modular Approach:

  • Modular Architecture: Design custom developments as independent modules that can be deployed, updated, and managed separately from the core system. This modular approach simplifies maintenance and upgrades.
  • Decoupled Applications: Keep custom applications decoupled from the core ERP to reduce dependencies and potential conflicts during system upgrades.

 

5. Continuous Integration and Deployment (CI/CD):

  • Automated Testing: Implement CI/CD pipelines with automated testing to ensure that custom developments and extensions do not negatively impact the core system.
  • Frequent Updates: Use CI/CD practices to frequently update and deploy side-by-side extensions, ensuring they remain aligned with the core system updates and enhancements.

 

6. Governance and Best Practices:

  • Change Management: Establish robust governance frameworks to manage changes to the SAP system, ensuring adherence to the clean core principle.
  • Documentation and Standards: Maintain comprehensive documentation and enforce coding standards and best practices for all custom developments and extensions.

 

7. SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI):

  • Integration Capabilities: Use CPI for seamless integration between SAP and non-SAP systems. CPI provides pre-built connectors and integration flows that simplify the integration process.
  • Data Consistency and Synchronisation: Ensure consistent data flow and synchronisation between the core system and external applications through reliable integration mechanisms.

 

8. Regular System Audits:

  • Audit Custom Code: Conduct regular audits of custom code and extensions to identify and remediate any deviations from the clean core principle.
  • Performance Monitoring: Monitor the performance of the core system and extensions to detect and address any issues promptly.

 

Immediate Benefits for Organisation Adapting Clean Core Design

 

SAP Clean Core is a design approach to keep the core of the SAP ERP system, particularly SAP S/4HANA, free from custom modifications and custom code staying as close to what SAP ships in its packaged enterprise application. This approach emphasises the use of standard SAP functionalities and extensions through side-by-side developments using the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), staying current, ensuring that the core system remains intact and easier to maintain. All custom adaptations can be done in the vicinity of the core in the surrounding applications, keeping the core pristine.

 

1. Simplified Maintenance and Upgrades:

  • Easier Upgrades: By adapting clean extensibility, companies can more easily adopt new SAP updates and enhancements by avoiding custom modifications to the core system. This reduces the complexity and cost associated with system upgrades.
  • Reduced Downtime: With fewer customisations in the core, the risk of disruptions during upgrades is minimised, leading to less downtime and clean day to day operations.

 

2. Improved Stability and Performance:

  • Reliable System: A clean core system tends to be more stable and reliable since it is less likely to be affected by issues arising from custom code. Clean data and Clean process will leverage SAP recommended best practices with relevant used data.
  • Optimised Performance: Standard SAP solutions are optimised for performance. Keeping the core stay current on a clean software stack, clean helps maintain this performance level.

 

3. Enhanced Security:

  • Consistent Security Standards: Standard SAP functionalities adhere to rigorous security standards. Reducing custom code, leveraging clean interface points minimises potential security vulnerabilities.
  • Faster Patch Application: Security patches can be applied more swiftly without the need to test extensive custom modifications. All cloud products have in-built security features meeting regulatory needs.

 

4. Cost Efficiency:

  • Lower Development Costs: Developing custom code can be expensive and time-consuming. Maintaining them throughout their lifecycle costs you more. Leveraging standard functionalities reduces these costs.
  • Decreased Support and Maintenance Costs: A clean core reduces the burden on IT support teams and lowers ongoing maintenance expenses. 

 

5. Agility and Flexibility:

  • Rapid Deployment: New functionalities and processes can be deployed more quickly when relying on standard SAP solutions and extensions through BTP. Surrounding SAP ecosystems products like SAP AI Core and SAP Cloud Analytics are compatible with S/4HANA and maintaining them becomes seamless.
  • Scalability: The clean core approach facilitates easier scaling of the system to meet growing business needs.

 

Challenges Organisation Need to Overcome Adapting Clean Core Design

 

The SAP Clean Core approach offers substantial benefits, including easier maintenance, improved stability, enhanced security, cost efficiency, and greater agility. However, it also presents challenges such as the initial transition effort, limited customisation options, the need for specialised skills, integration complexity, and potential vendor dependency.

Organisations must carefully weigh these factors and plan strategically to maximise the benefits of a clean core while addressing its challenges effectively. The challenges that businesses and organisations adapting it will need to consider in each of these five areas are mentioned below.

 

1.    Initial Transition Effort:

  • Refactoring Existing Customisations: Transitioning to a clean core may require significant effort to refactor or replace existing custom code and modifications.
  • Change Management: Ensuring that the organisation is aligned with the clean core approach can be challenging, particularly in large enterprises with complex systems.

 

2.    Limited Customisation:

  • Functional Gaps: Some unique business processes may not be fully supported by standard SAP functionalities, necessitating creative solutions or workarounds.
  • Dependence on Extensions: Companies may need to rely heavily on extensions and side-by-side developments, which can introduce complexity integration and management.

 

3.    Skill Requirements:

  • Need for Specialised Skills: Utilising SAP BTP for side-by-side extensions requires specific technical expertise in cloud platform services and SAP integration technologies.
  • Training and Development: Organisations may need to invest in training and development to up-skill their IT teams on new methodologies and tools.

 

4.    Integration Complexity:

  • Seamless Integration: Ensuring seamless integration between the core SAP system and side-by-side extensions can be complex, requiring robust integration strategies and tools.
  • Data Consistency: Maintaining data consistency and integrity across integrated systems is a critical challenge.

 

5.    Vendor Dependency:

  • Reliance on SAP Roadmap: Organisations adopting the clean core approach may become more dependent on SAP's roadmap and updates, potentially limiting flexibility.
  • Third-Party Solutions: Balancing the use of third-party solutions with the clean core philosophy can be challenging, as integrating these solutions may reintroduce some complexity.

 

By embracing the SAP Clean Core design principle, organisations can achieve a more agile, maintainable, and cost-effective SAP landscape, ensuring long-term success and adaptability in a rapidly changing business environment. You would need a trusted partner, a system integrator, who can service you in each of these discussed areas. Click on the link to know more about SAP Clean Core Strategy.

entry.title: Setting up Trial SAP Datasphere Account with Trial SAP SAC Live Connection
entry.content:
Prerequisites:-

  • Create your SAP Datasphere account and SAP Analytics account using your personal or business email.
  • Create a space for your projects and add users in Datasphere.
  • Keep the space name and tenant host link handy.

Now, click on Product Switch and jump to SAP Analytics Cloud:

 

You will be jumped into the SAP Analytics Cloud home page:

Go to the Connection tab to create a live connection with your Datasphere account to readily use the data and build innovative and beautiful dashboards.

Click on New Connection and select SAP Datasphere:

1. Now copy the space name in Datasphere as a name, and you can enter any description of your choice (free text).

2. Under the Connection Details section, enter the tenant details we have in the first step (highlighted till) without https:// and HTTPS Post as '443' by default.

3. Authentication  Method as SAML Single Sign on and click Ok 

 

Now your connection will be created and you can find it in Connections .

In this way we can test the connection between Trail SAP Datasphere and Trail SAP Analytics Cloud and have a user experience.

By creating a live connection of SAP Datasphere tenant with SAC, we can build stories directly against the data that is modeled in SAP Datasphere. Live connections between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere can be established across tenants and data centers.

entry.title: First Half 2024 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile
entry.content:
In an era where smartphones are ubiquitous and workforces are increasingly mobile, the role of technology in Human Resources (HR) is more crucial than ever. At SAP SuccessFactors we want to make a meaningful, positive impact on the work life of every SuccessFactors user wherever they are working from by anticipating their most critical needs at the right time and exploit the unique mobile capabilities they’ve come to love from consumer apps for all mobile users.

For the 1H 2024 release, we put our investment in areas that matter most for mobile users, especially from an employee experience perspective, which is based on historical usage data and the features that benefit deskless workers. We are excited to share with you reimagined features such as the SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central people profile and org chart. Additionally, on the SAP SuccessFactors Learning side we have built richer functions for the Integrated Learning Experience (ILX), as well as offline learning support on the Android platform.

Employee Central

Two new people profile experiences include the profile preview and spotlight view. With the profile preview, employees can edit their basic information, upload an “About me Video”, and much more. The spotlight view helps them to build their own brand by showcasing things like skills, competencies, and mentoring experiences. Watch this video to learn more about the new people profile experiences.

With the org chart, we have enhanced the overall navigation. Available on mobile, employees can quickly navigate to these new views and explore their organizational structure with improved integration with new people profile page. Watch this video to learn more about the new org chart experiences.

To make it easier for employees and managers to access information, we have also delivered more Employee Central (EC) quick actions such as View Job Relationship, View Phone, View Email – just to mention a few.

SAP SuccessFactors Learning

In the last release we introduced the new integrated Learning experience (ILX). With this release we have enhanced the new Learning experience in the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile iOS and Android mobile apps with Important Learning, Requests for Me, curricula requirements, and filter options. We've also relocated the new Learning experience switch and updated the new Learning experience information message.

Also with this release, we now support the ability to download learning content to launch offline in the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile Android app.

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits

Customers who are using EC Global Benefits can enjoy an entirely new enrollment experience for Insurance and Savings Plans, which includes a step-by-step, guided flow, side-by-side plan comparisons as well as relevant help at each step.

SAP SuccessFactors Performance Management

Mobile users can view and provide ratings and comments for skills, add skills to the form, or delete them as well as view and edit weights for skills and the skill section.

I hope that this has given you helpful insights into what we delivered in our 1H 2024 release for the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile Application.

For more details please check the release note here .

Please read this blog to learn when you can try new feature in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app.

For information on release highlights across the SAP SuccessFactors HXM suite, see the Release Highlights Overview blog by Carrie Klauss and don’t miss Bianka Woelke’s release video .

The new released content can be downloaded through the Apple App Store and Google Play Store. For users in China, the app can be downloaded through the Tencent App Store.

More information on the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile App you can find in our SAP SuccessFactors community here.

And, read my blog on how to Get Started with SAP SuccessFactors Mobile App.

SAP SuccessFactors Mobile is a native app, which is available on iOS as well as on Android. It provides employees and managers access to HR activities anywhere at any time. The app is free of charge and can be used by every SAP SuccessFactors customer.

Where to find more information

  • sap.com
  • Overview video
  • View documentation to help you get started

entry.title: Re: add/ read attachments of freight order
entry.content: But in this case there is no data stored in table SRGBTBREL when adding an attachment. Therefore I think that a different technique is used here.

entry.title: Re: Join types SAP queries
entry.content: Thank you for this wonderful blog, really helpful.

entry.title: Pricing problem in Commerce Cloud with CPS
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

we are currently facing an issue with the VCP/CPS-Engine Integration for configurable products into SAP Commerce Cloud.

In the log we can see that prices are found and returned by the webservices.
We also configured everything in the backoffice:

Pricing options:

Show base price and selected options:

Pricing Details:

But during the configuration, no prices are shown.

But they are shown on the summary page of the configuration before the configuration is added to the cart.

What might be missing here?

Best regards
Benedikt

entry.title: Re: SIPT_LIKP - CDS View??
entry.content:
Hi @inesmartins 

The table SIPT_LIKP is included in the CDS view I_PT_DigitalSignature but unfortunately its not released 

for customer use .

 

Please follow the KBA 2963059 ( https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2963059 ) and submit 

a feature request if you would like it released 

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Setting up an ABAP Environment on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Overview

We will set up the ABAP Environment on SAP BTP by using the Cloud Foundry runtime environment.

1. Add a Service Plan

Go to your Subaccount.
Add an new Service Plan and search for "ABAP environment".
Select all 3 options for the available plans.

2. Service Marketplace

Then navigate to the "Service Marketplace" and Search for "ABAP environment".
Now you are ready to configure the ABAP environment.

3. Create a New Instance

We need to create a new instance to deploy the ABAP environment. So let's do this.

Go through the 3-Step procedure

Step 1

Step 2

Step 3

If everything is correct you will see the following message. Click on "View Instance" for more details.

Under Instances you can see that the new ABAP environment have the status "Creation in progress".

That's it.

entry.title: Re: F4 Help for file path via Fiori App doesn't allow selection from local machine
entry.content:
Hi,

I am facing similar issue, did you get any solution for this?

Thanks.

entry.title: out of memory while calling a procedure from xs job
entry.content:
Hello HANA Experts,

We have a procedure which fetches data from HANA calculation view and inserts into a table. Calling a procedure from studio sql editor works fine. However calling a procedure via xs job results out of memory issue. Does the procedure gets executed in different mode when called from xs job?  is there any hint we can use to trigger the way it gets triggered from sql editor?

Below is the exception log

search table error: cannot allocate enough memory:  [9] Memory allocation failed;exception 1000002: Allocation failed ; $failure_type$=STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT_FROM_GLOBAL_CONFIG; $failure_flag$=; $size$=2627173632; $name$=Results; $type$=pool; $inuse_count$=263; $allocated_size$=35515423384; $alignment$=8
,Exception in executor plan11765910@xxx while executing pop 9 (SQLScript function CA_BOE:CA.APPS.COMMON::CA_UNIQUE_REALM_TBF, line 10; SQLScript function CA_BOE:CA.SDI.APPS.TABLE_FUNCTIONS::D_PROJ_INFO_TF, line 28; SQLScript function CA_BOE:CA.SDI.APPS.TABLE_FUNCTIONS::D_TIME_TF, line 29; SQLScript function CA_BOE:CA.SDI.APPS.TABLE_FUNCTIONS::D_UNSPSC_TF, line 99; SQLScript function CA_BOE:CA.SDI.APPS.TABLE_FUNCTIONS::D_USER_DATA_TF, line 67; SQLScript function CA_BOE:CA.SDI.APPS.TABLE_FUNCTIONS::F_CUR_CNF_TF, line 32; SQLScript function CA_BOE:CA.SDI.APPS.TABLE_FUNCTIONS::F_CUR_VAR_TF, line 15; SQLScript function CA_BOE:CA.SDI.APPS.TABLE_FUNCTIONS::F_GEN_CON_W_TF, line 187; SQLScript function CA_BOE:CA.SDI.APPS.TABLE_FUNCTIONS::F_SRC_PROJ_TF, line 323; SQLScript function CA_BOE:CA.SDI.APPS.TABLE_FUNCTIONS::F_USR_ABLTY_TF, line 37; SQLScript function CA_BOE:CA.SDI.APPS.TABLE_FUNCTIONS::J_OWNRS_PRJ_TF, line 13) at SQLScript/Executor/Evaluator/SeEvaluatorImpl.cpp:725

 

regards,

Vinoth

entry.title: Tax Jurisdiction does not come up as default in the Maintenance Order
entry.content:
When I create a Plant Maintenance Order, the Tax Jurisdiction is not coming as a default and we have to enter it manually. The code is maintained for all the Planning and Maint Plants in OX10. 

 

Is there some setting that I am missing?

entry.title: Re: How to attach documents to a Journal Entry within SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud from ABAP.
entry.content:
Hi @xaviercarbo10, nice to meet you.

We had a simmilar problem. It's quite strange, but you need to know that the communication users authorizations depends on the communication agreements assigned to them, but there is no information about this on API HUB.

The authorization problem must be solved by implementing another communication scenario that also includes the Attachment API too, to find the right one try this:

1. Go to application Display Inbound Services and filter by Inbound Service ID = 

API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV_0001_IWSG.
 
2. Display the list of communication scenarios that includes this inbound service.
 
3. Create a new communication agreement for the most appropriate communication scenario for your business object, using the same user.
 
Let me know if works! 🤞
 
PTI: After find the correct one, you can remove the communication arrangement exposed in my post (SAP_COM_0002), if you dont need it 😉.

entry.title: Re: certification check for an operation activity in Orde...
entry.content:
Hi Anvesh , 

You can assign certification to operation activity . Use Manage Certification app and select the certification and navigate to assignment , you have option to assign Resource , Material and Operation Activity to a certification.

Hope this helps.

Thanks

Rahul

entry.title: Restrict only J2ee_adm_sid periodic password change in solution manager JAVA
entry.content:
Hi All,

We use the java_adm_sid user to access the Solution Manager JAVA stack. Our requirement is to ensure that the password for the java_adm_sid user does not expire, as it currently prompts for periodic changes. How can we enforce this setting?

If we update the password, where do we need to ensure the latest password for j2ee_Admin is updated in Solution Manager?

Regards

Appuru

entry.title: How to get the list of SAP BW queries used in a AFO workbook saved in SAP BI Launchpad
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have several SAP AFO workbooks saved in the SAP BI Launchpad, and I need to obtain a list of workbooks along with their associated SAP BW queries. In SAP BW, we had access to this list, which was extremely useful. However, I haven't been able to extract this information using SAP BI Launchpad.

I've tried using the Query Builder, but I couldn't generate a list of data sources (BW query technical names) for each workbook. I also attempted to use the SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform without success.

Has anyone successfully retrieved this list from the CMC (AFO workbooks and their corresponding BW queries)?

Thank you,
César Sevilha

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud Access using IAS as Proxy but no user in the identity store
entry.content:
We have a customer requirement to use Okta as the Corporate Identity Provider for all cloud applications including SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud with IAS as a Proxy. The customer does not want users to be created in the Cloud Identity Store and managed through the Corporate Identity Store. We have tested this solution and it appears to work. However, we are not sure if this will work for all the SAP cloud applications such as S/4HANA Public Cloud, Identity and Access Governance etc.

Does anyone have experience implementing such a use case? 

Regards,

Subbu Iyer

entry.title: Re: SAC 实时连接中 怎么样将组织结构设置成层级结构呢
entry.content: 应该是可以，business content里好像有demo，你找找看呢

entry.title: Deploy Card as App in Build Work Zone with Work Zone BAS Extension
entry.content:
Background

From the SAP Build Work Zone, the advanced edition U2404 Release, Cards are now supported as visualization types of local apps and are maintained in the Content Manager. 

From SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition U2406 Release, Deploy Card as an app is supported In Work Zone BAS Extension.

Deploy Card as an App

Auto-generate AppID

Following the card development guidelines, you can try to create a card project as before. Right-click manifest.json, and you can see the button "UI Integration Card: Deploy to SAP Build Work Zone".

Select the destination that you configured. Refer to destination configuration

After the deployment, you will find the auto-generated app.properties file under the .card folder as below. And the appId is defined in the app.properties file.

Go to Content Manager, you can find the app is created with the id defined in the app.properties file.

The app is automatically added to the Everyone Role if it is the first deployment.

Go to Work Zone Admin Console, you can see card and you need to enable it before using it.

Customized AppID

Following the same structure of the auto-generated appId, you can create the app.properties file under the .card folder and fill in the appId as you want. To keep consistent, we recommend using the V4 uuid format. 

Override Card Version 

The upgrade card version is no longer mandatory. You can choose to downgrade or upgrade the card version. 

Change Card ID or App ID

After the deployment is done, if you want to change to another card ID, you need to also change the appId. Otherwise, you will get the error saying the "App ID is associated with another card". Or you can choose to delete the app in Content Manager. If you want to change an App ID, you also need to change the card ID.

Everyone Role

The app will be assigned to Everyone Role automatically only if it is the first deployment. For example, if you remove the Everyone Role after the first deployment, the Everyone Role will no longer be assigned in the subsequent deployment.

entry.title: Request to provide real time examples on Odata - Association and Navigation
entry.content:
Hi All,

After 3 years of career gap, I started learning few technical aspects(one of those is Odata) to resume my work in SAP ABAP. Please provide few real time example on Odata - Association and Navigation? This would help me to understand more on the concept and helps me in attending the interview.

Thanks in Advance.

Hi Rahul, 

As per this blog Worker Certification Check using Production Proces... - SAP Community

for the work center pod I can assign it to a start button 

but in Order pod I do not have any button and its a standard plugin to start and complete operation with Plugin "Operation/Phase List"

regards,

Anvesh

entry.title: Re: Receiver Port in incoming IDoc in ECC
entry.content:
For sender port check SAP Note 1653715 - Default sender port for Java IDOC Adapter receiver channels:

Java IDoc Adapter managed connection factory and could be set using NWA in Application Resources tool. Select JavaIDOCAdapter Resource Adapter -> Properties Tab and edit the value for the DefaultSenderPort property

entry.content: Kok4 not available to schedule background job for infinite time line

entry.title: Asset Manager - Digital signature not supported on ERP
entry.content:
We have installed ERP with Mobile addons:

  • SMFND 630_740 0013 SAPK-63013INSMFND
  • SMERP 630_740 0013 SAPK-63013INSMERP (SAM 2310)

I want to configure Digital signature for SAP Asset Manager. When a person finishes a work order, he signs the work order (standard/default behavior in mobile application).

I followed this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-asset-manager-enable-digital-signature-for-work-order-headers/ba-p/13492690

In syclo, when I select oMDO SAM2310_DSIG_CONFIGURATION or SAM2310_DSIG_COMMIT I get error:

Error: oMDO handler /MERP/CL_PM_DSIG_COMMIT_OD no longer exist.

Error: oMDO handler /MERP/CL_PM_DSIGN_CONFIG_OD no longer exist.

oMDO Handlers (ABAP code) are missing in the system. I do not have those problems (errors/missing code) on S/4HANA systems.

entry.title: Hyperlink from Field to Group
entry.content:
Hi,

I haven't posted before, so hopefully I'm in the right place. I am using Crystal Reports for Visual Studio 3.0.24 with Visual Studio 2017. I'm trying to make a very simple report using Project Data / .NET Objects as the report data source. I have nothing but a single field from the data and a group field. I am trying to hyperlink from the field to a related group, and I think I've got everything setup correctly except that I cannot figure out how to use the Select From field in the hyperlink options.

I right-clicked on the group field and clicked copy. I then went into the field's format dialog to the hyperlink tab. I select the "Another Report Object" radio button, then I click Paste, and it fills in the Object Name and the Data Context values, but that would not narrow the link down to a single group. This leaves the Select From field, which I cannot figure out how to use. I have tried using the formula to return the value of the group name. I'm not sure what else to try.

The final result is that the report doesn't actually have any hyperlinks in it. None of the fields react to a click at all. It seems like this should be relatively simple. Are there other settings that I'm not using correctly? I couldn't find any explanation for what the Select From field should contain.

Thanks in advance for any help!

entry.title: Re: Qm instection lot status "MCAN"
entry.content: Hi Herman, there is no option to put the status MCAN through user profile. Can you please let me know where else can i look.

entry.title: Re: certification check for an operation activity in Orde...
entry.content: Hi Anvesh , you can invoke the production process using Manage Automatic trigger > Business Rules. Create a business rule while Selecting Business Service like "Start SFC" and appropriate Trigger Point . assign Action to same production process and map the input parameter accordingly. Thanks Rahul

entry.title: Re: Questions on 'Just Ask' in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
Hi Animikhc,
Thanks for these questions. I know many in SAP Analytics community have the same so its great that you give me the opportunity to answer these.

 

  1. Contextual Awareness: 
    Just ask is about NLQ, questions & answers, the context of previous questions is not used. This is one of the key values we expect from Joule. The conversation context will enable to iterate and not have to reset it in the next question. A context aware conversations will also  enable to ask the user to refine its questions and remove ambiguities.

  2. Customizable LLM Model: 
    just ask LLM generates analytic queries from text. For this it is trained on synthetic dataset that do not contain any customer data or metadata. The training datasets consist of generalized abstract queries. So having just ask trained with a company specific queries is not relevant. To ground  just ask LLM query generation just ask performs text similarity searches on the indexed data models and process the result via a named entity recognition model (NER)
  3. just ask  vs Joule 
    Joule adds the conversation context to just ask as mentioned above. 
    Joule in SAP Analytics Cloud is planned to be a story assistant, it will be available both at view time and at design time. At design time it will support content developers not only to generate visualizations leveraging just ask NLQ, it will  also help to generate story scripts, calculations and more.
    Beyond SAP Analytics Cloud, Joule is planned to be available across SAP applications portfolio: https://www.sap.com/mena/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-assistant.html,
    Joule will serve analytical answers in SAP cloud applications thanks to just ask technology

  4. Underlying LLM Model: 
    just ask LLM came from askdata the company that SAP acquired in 2 years ago. https://news.sap.com/2022/07/sap-acquires-askdata/
    It's proprietary transformer uses an encoder-decoder architecture. It is hosted in SAP Analytics Cloud backend, no data leaves SAP Analytics Cloud host, no calls to public LLMs.

entry.title: Re: Error handling? What error handling?
entry.content:
While we're in the confessional...

As a student, we were learning to write compilers in C (This was before C++. Yes. I am that old), on Sun workstations. They were networked together and we discovered that we could log on to another workstation without any further credential checks. Mostly, we were using command lines. We'd type something in, it would execute on the other computer, and the results would come to ours.

Then we discovered that any commands that produced graphical output turned up on the suborned workstation, not ours. This enabled jolly japes like flashing up a password screen on some unsuspecting student and thereby gain access to all their files.

We also discovered how to mirror text output, so anything the remote system typed appeared on both our screens, likewise anything we typed. I recall we had some poor sap (not SAP) convinced that he was playing a (text based) adventure game...

I had a little program that bounced a ball across the screen. I'd run it every now and then on someone else's workstation and watch them jump. Then I thought it would be fun to do it on every workstation on the network, to cries of "oh come on, we're trying to work here".

Then I though how about if every bounce spawns two balls that bounce. On every workstation.

Oops. And I'm not talking about a programming paradigm.

I was young and foolish and was never caught. I never did it again - even being old and foolish.

entry.title: Re: A coding contest over coffee?!
entry.content:
Since we're only allowed one, I've added another incident as a reply to mine.

I'm a fine law-abiding citizen now. Honest!

entry.title: Re: Killer Cycle

Thanks and Regards,

Anvesh

entry.title: Re: The future of Blockchain & SAP

entry.title: Unable to manage LOVC dependencies in S4 HANA public cloud:
entry.content:
Hello all,

I have a requirement to add an object dependency to BOM items. As far as my knowledge goes, we cannot use AVC for that. We need to use LOVC here with processing mode as Classic.

When I try to create a dependency for a material using PMEVC, by default it is created as AVC and it does not let me switch to Classic:

 

Now, I could not find an equivalent of transaction CU01 in S4 Public Cloud where I can create a new object dependency with processing mode as Classic.

The only work around I found was to use the environment section on the left side panel of PMEVC where I can create a Classic dependency:

 

 

 

 

Now I can save it, but I cannot access it after that through PMEVC as system does not allow me to assign this to any configuration profile:

 

The only way I can access this is by using the app “Variant Configuration Overview”:

 

 

But the challenge here is, even if I try to modify this, I get the error message that I don’t have the access to CU02 which is not a public cloud application:

 

So, my question is, am I using the right approach? If yes, how can I resolve this issue of CU02 access?

If my approach is not correct, how do we work with classic object dependency (LOVC as we know it) in S4 Public Cloud?

Thank you

Snigdhodeep

entry.title: Re: Hyperlink from Field to Group
entry.content:
Hello, I believe that you would need to be using the DHTML viewer in an Enterprise environment (e.g. BOBJ or CR Server) in order for this to work. Please see the Report Part navigation section in help.sap.com.

"This navigation is available only in the DHTML viewers (zero-client, server-side viewers)."

entry.title: Re: How to display landed cost of multiped PO
entry.content: Dear Gaurav-chauhan, We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no further updates under the topic. You can unaccept it anytime if the answer provided was not helpful enough and you have further questions. Best regards, Geovana

entry.title: Re: certification check for an operation activity in Orde...
entry.content:
Error seems the user which is trying to start the operation activity does not have required certification assigned . Check the manage user assignment app for the certificate assignment .

Note :

a. compare the User Id in both the flow , it might be you are passing different id in input parameter.

b. Manage automatic trigger uses technical service user to invoke the production process , which might not be same as operator 

So this way you wont be having the current operator user context in production process. You can query the SFC and check if it provides the operator context in production process and use that userid rather technical service user.

Otherwise another option to customize the standard Order POD  ( copy and change ) and enable a button using POD designer . You can assign an action in same same group of action where "Good receipt" posting is available, this is with low config effort.

Thanks

Rahul

entry.title: Re: SAP Plant Maintenance (EAM) Integration with SAP Extended Warehouse Management in S/4 HANA 2022
entry.content: Nice Blog @SaikiranGummadi  Very informative

entry.title: Re: Custom Tasks in Onboarding and Offboarding
entry.content: @DP4  - is the questionnaire within the custom task a document? That is the only way a document would be tied to a custom task within Profile. A custom task itself does not generate a form at this time however we are looking to provide the ability to tie a document flow to custom tasks in future.

entry.title: Re: Unable to manage LOVC dependencies in S4 HANA public...
entry.content:
Hi @snigdhodeep 

 

In S/4HANA Cloud only Advanced Variant Configuration is available.

In an Onpremise system or Private Cloud the customer has the possibility to select classical VC or AVC but 

this is not possible in Cloud .

 

The transaction CU02 is not available in Cloud as an app and PMEVC needs to be used instead.

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: Outbound Communication to S4 On-Premise(OData) from B...
entry.content: You checked the wrong log: the "http_access.log" records only UI requests from the browser to port 8443, it has nothing to do with http requests to backend systems. As I said, these can be seen only in the Payload Trace. (Files traffic_trace_xxxx.trc.)

entry.title: Re: How to select BOM components in sales order based on...
entry.content: One quick follow up question (sorry) - is Classical VC (LO-VC) available in S/4HANA Cloud Public? I have not seen any option equivalent to CU01.

entry.title: Create business user API behavior
entry.content:
Hi,

I am creating business user using below API

querybusinessuserin


It creates business user. Also employee is getting created with above payload.

Is this expected behavior?

What change is needed if we don't want employee to get created with business user?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Create business user API behavior
entry.content: A Business User of the new SAP S/4HANA model is a Business Partner of role type Employee assigned to a SU01-user (workforce). Business Partner is the leading object. Therefore it is not supported to create just SU01-Users (Employee alone). A lot applications rely on this model and would not work anymore. There are no mechanisms currently to create 'Employee' record alone.

entry.title: Can we Activate Budget Availability Control for ACDOCP Table for Projects in S/4 HANA private Cloud?
entry.content:
We have SAP PPM in our landscape and a project is created in PS via a PPM Item.

If we refer this latest blog by @andrea_langlotz : Portfolio Management – Enhanced Financial Planning... - SAP Community, which sayes "With the recent SAP S/4HANA releases the integration capabilities of Financial Planning for Portfolio Management have been enhanced to support integration into the SAP S/4HANA periodic financial planning, i.e. ACDOCP based planning"

Which means we can use ACDOCP table to store the Budget values planned via PPM item as well in ACDOCP table with the correct plan category.

Question:

1) Can we Activate Budget Availability Control for ACDOCP Table for Projects in S/4 HANA 2023 private Cloud?

2) If yes, can we achive functionalities of Buget Transfer/Budget release/Budget supplimentals also from ACDOCP table?

3) If SAC is used for budget planning, are the values stored in ACDOCP Table and can we use the availability control from SAC in that case ?

4) Can we completely get rid of Old tables and move to combination of ACDOCP Table & ACDOCA tables going forward ?

Or can anyone please explain how is sap planning the Budget Availability Control for ACDOCP Table via ACDOCP Table?

Thanks in advance for the help!

entry.title: Re: From FSD document in TM to Create PO in ERP with other than item category U
entry.content: Hello Gurus: After modifying MM to accept service category K from TM, only the PO is generated but the Service Order is not created. If you know what else we should move the MM? I appreciate your comments

entry.title: Print a text block if there is more than x amount of products on my quotation in Crystal Reports
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm very new to SAP and Crystal Reports so i'll try to explain my issue as best as I can.

I've been building a Quotation form for my employer in Crystal Reports. The quotation pulls Article codes from SAP on a Quotation request into my report. Everything works perfectly but as soon as there are more than 4 products in the request, the 5th (and 6th and 7th and so on) will print to the next page (as they should).

On the first page I have headers (which are text blocks) for the products. These headers are built in Page header B. Which I can set to display only on page 1 or multiple pages.

Now here comes the problem. There is also a big text block in my report foot section after my details sections. This text block HAS to be in the report so my report will always consist of 2 pages. 
I want to make it so that when my quotation has more than 4 products it prints the Page header B on page 2 aswel, but if it has 4 or less products it shouldn't print it on any other page than page 1.

If it's possible at all, how could I implement this in my report? I was thinking to use an if statement to use in the Page header A section but i've got no clue what to write or what statment to put there.

If it's not possible then i'm open for any suggestions how I could fix this.

entry.title: Re: Deactivated workflow for journal entry but validation...
entry.content:
Hi mathieu7,

The validations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud are created in the following apps:

Manage Substitution Validation Rules Journal Entries app

Custom Logic app

You can check what is active and deactivate it.

Don't forget to check if the deactivation has no further impacts on more processes.

Best regards

Edna

Thanks,

Anvesh

entry.title: Re: Outbound Communication to S4 On-Premise(OData) from B...
entry.content: @ Ulrich_Schmidt First screenshot above is from "traffic_trace_xxxx.trc"

entry.title: How to create technical users in SAP cloud
entry.content:
How to create technical user and assign them roles for computer or system? It is required to distinguish users between technical users and business users. And currently I only found business roles in the system.

For example, there is a computer and everyone use this computer can logon the SAP system.

Another situation is that OA system can do some activities in SAP system, and it is not employee.

So how can I create user ID for these 2 situations?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create technical users in SAP cloud
entry.content:
Technical users can only be created by customers as type communication user or printing user. There are apps for communication users and for printing users. Maintain business user app is only for business users. Some of the examples i have put it below. It is not possible to create other technical users in cloud system.

 

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002792641


https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/user-management-overview-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13322516


https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/printing-in-the-sap-s-4hana-cloud-system/ba-p/12838853

entry.title: Re: Unable to manage LOVC dependencies in S4 HANA public...
entry.content: Thank you so much for your response. Where I am struggling is, in S4 Public Cloud, I can still see Object Dependencies with Classic mode as a part of the best practices scenario. This is available in the app Variant Configuration Overview. I have seen those even work (like selecting the right BOM component during MRP run) but those are not modifiable as system calls for CU02. Does this mean, although those elements are there, those are not modifiable at all?

entry.title: $100 OFF Temu Coupon Code (acp856709) or (act200019)
entry.content:
All countries, including the US, UK, Canada, and Mexico, can enjoy a $100 welcome bonus at Temu by using the coupon code (acp856709) and a 90 off discount on product purchases with code (act200019).

Create an organization and add member accounts

If you have a management account or member account of an organization in AWS Organizations, then skip this section.

If you don't have an organization in AWS Organizations, then create an organization. Make sure that the organization includes the account that you use when you create a private re:Post. You can create an organization using the AWS Management Console, a command from the AWS CLI, or a command from the SDK APIs.

Note: If you receive errors when running AWS CLI commands, make sure that you're using the most recent version of the AWS CLI .

To create an organization using the AWS Management Console, follow these steps:

  1. Sign in with your IAM user or role, and then open the AWS Organizations console.
  2. Choose Create an organization.

By default, your organization is created with all features active. If AWS didn't previously verify your management account, AWS sends a verification email to the email address that's associated with your management account. Within 24 hours, follow the instructions in that email to verify your email address. After AWS verifies your email address, you can invite member accounts to join your organization. 

Enable and configure AWS IAM Identity Center for your account

  • If you haven't already enabled IAM Identity Center, do so in the same Region where you want to create a private re:Post. To enable an organization instance of IAM Identity Center, follow the directions in Enabling AWS IAM Identity Center .

    Note: re:Post Private supports only organization instances of IAM Identity Center .

  • Configure IAM Identity Center for your account. For more information, see Get started with common tasks in IAM Identity Center .

Create an IAM role for AWS Support case creation and management (optional)

Note: You need to create this IAM role only if you want to create AWS Support cases from questions on re:Post Private.

  1.  Create an IAM role with the required permissions to create, manage, and resolve AWS Support cases for you. The re:Post Private service uses this role to make API calls to AWS Support. You choose this IAM role when you create your private re:Post in the re:Post Private console.
  2. Attach an IAM policy to this role so that the role has the required permissions to complete actions related to AWS Support case creation and management.

For more information, see Managing access to AWS Support case creation and management in re:Post Private .

Create a private re:Post

To create a new private re:Post, follow these steps:

  1. Open the re:Post Private console .

  2. On the console's homepage, choose Create private re:Post.

  3. On the Create private re:Post page, for Pricing, select Free tier or Standard tier based on your use case. Note that if you already used Free Tier for your account, the Free tier option isn't available to you.

  4. Under Details, follow these steps:

    For Name, enter a unique name for your private re:Post.

    (Optional) For Description, enter a brief description for your private re:Post.

    For Custom subdomain, enter a custom name for your subdomain.

  5. (Optional) To customize your data encryption settings, under Data encryption, select Customize encryption settings. Then, follow either of these steps:

    For Choose an AWS KMS key, select an AWS Key Management Service key or an Amazon Resource Name (ARN).
    -or-
    Choose Create an AWS KMS key. Then, create the AWS KMS key .

  6. (Optional) Under Service access for Support case integration, select Enable service access for this re:Post.

    Note: You can also turn on this option after you create the private re:Post.

    For Please select an existing IAM role below or create a new role in IAM console, use the search bar to find your existing IAM role.

    -or-

    Choose create a new role in IAM console.

    If you create a new role, then follow the instructions in Create an IAM role .

    If you use an existing service role, then in the search bar enter the ARN of the role that you want to use. Choose the role from the dropdown list. This is the role that you created in the section Create an IAM role for AWS Support case creation and management.

  7. (Optional) Under Tags, choose Add new tag. Then, enter the following information:

    For Key, enter your custom tag key.

    For Value, enter your custom tag value.

    To add more tags, choose Add new tag.

  8. Choose Create this re:Post.

A confirmation page notifies you that your private re:Post is being created. You can view the status of the private re:Post in the Status field. When your private re:Post is created, the Status field shows Creating.

It takes about 30 minutes for the private re:Post to be created.

When your private re:Post is ready, the Status field shows Online. To access your private re:Post, use the AWS generated subdomain for your private re:Post that's listed under the Settings tab. After your AWS account team completes the review, you can view the Custom subdomain for your private re:Post under the Settings tab.

entry.title: Temu Coupon {act977950} or {acq758919} $100 Off For This Month All User...
entry.content:
SAP Community

This plateform offering an exceptional $100 discount to new and existing customers across all countries, including the US, UK, Canada, and Mexico. $100 off Temu Coupon Code [taj58794] or ( acq758919) for New USER. The Temu coupon code taj58794 or acq758919 offers a $100 discount for new users. This offer is exclusive for new users in the USA and can be used to buy a wide range of products from Temu's platform.

To expedite communication, we’ve been tracking common questions and complaints to address them collectively. In this post, I’ll cover several recurring topics to let you know that we’ve heard you and to inform you of our plans moving forward.

Sorting Tag Feeds/Notifications by Content Type

Currently, all tag content is grouped under "posts," which includes both blogs and questions. We recognize the need for members to separate blog content from questions at the tag level. We're working on a solution to provide this option and will share more details as they become available.

Feeds by Publication Date (Not Just Recent Activity)
The "Recent Activity" feeds have caused confusion, with members expecting to see the most recently published posts. To clarify, "Recent Activity" includes any recent interactions with older posts. We understand the desire for chronological feeds of new questions and blog posts and are exploring ways to implement this feature. Updates will follow as we make progress.

Page Views on Blog Content
We acknowledge that blog posts should display the number of views and are working to reinstate this feature. More updates will be provided as we have them.

Blog Formatting Post-Migration
Due to the migration, some blog formatting changed, resulting in issues like excess white space and broken links. Editing old posts to restore previous formatting often worsens the issue and violates community best practices. We advise focusing on creating new content that adheres to current blogging standards, using the "Clear formatting" option when copying text from Word.

Subscriptions Not Triggering Notifications
Some members, including myself, have experienced issues with subscription notifications. While we investigate, you might try unsubscribing and resubscribing to your tags or adjusting your notification settings. We are working on resolving this problem and will update you as more information becomes available.

Character Restrictions in Display Names
We’re aware of complaints regarding character restrictions in display names and are exploring options to extend them. If your display name was truncated during migration, you can change it in the "My content" area of the site. For more details on display names and profile usernames, please refer to my post about profiles post-migration.

We anticipate more updates as we continue to refine the platform post-migration. Please subscribe to the "What's New" section to stay informed as my colleagues and I share further updates. Thank you for your patience and cooperation.
